items.title: New, harder to forge Malaysian passport rolling out June 1, public reminded not to use AI photos in applications
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 19 — The Immigration Department will begin issuing a new Malaysian passport with 94 security features from June 1, further strengthening the security of the nation’s identification document. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said that in the initial phase, the issuance of the new passport will involve four offices: the Immigration headquarters in Putrajaya; the Kuala Lumpur Passport Office in Jalan Duta; the Urban Transformation Centre (UTC) Wangsa Maju; and the Shah Alam Immigration Office. He said the rollout of the new passport will be implemented in stages, with full nationwide issuance expected by July.

items.title: Heineken Malaysia's Q1 net profit falls 14pct to RM104mil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Heineken Malaysia Bhd's net profit fell 14 per cent to RM104 million in the first quarter of financial year 2026 (Q1 FY26) from RM122 million due to softer consumer sentiment. Heineken Malaysia's revenue dropped 13 per cent to RM664 million from RM763.6 million, according to a filing to Bursa Malaysia. Managing director Martijn van Keulen said the quarterly performance reflected a more challenging operating environment, with softer consumer sentiment and geopolitical developments influencing spending patterns.

items.title: Pahang's river sand export issue raised during MB's meeting with minister in Putrajaya
items.summary: KUANTAN: Pahang Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail today paid a courtesy call on Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup. The meeting, held at Arthur's office in Putrajaya around noon today, is believed to have touched on matters related to river sand exports in the east coast state. During the Pahang assembly sitting last week, Wan Rosdy said he would be meeting Arthur to discuss efforts to revive river sand exports in a bid to boost state revenue.

items.title: Don't use AI to 'beautify' passport photos, Immigration warns
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Immigration Department has issued a stern warning to passport applicants against using Artificial Intelligence (AI) technology to "beautify" their application photos following the detection of several such cases recently. Director-General Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said while the department encouraged the use of online renewal services, some applicants had been caught submitting digitally manipulated images to improve their appearance. However, I must stress that applicants must not submit photos generated or enhanced by AI technology," Zakaria said at a press conference today.

items.title: Ringgit ends firmer vs major currencies, eases against greenback
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit ended mostly higher against major currencies but eased against the US dollar on Tuesday as market participants await further developments in Middle East negotiations between the United States (US) and Iran. At 6pm, the ringgit edged down to 3.9745/9785 versus the greenback from 3.9720/9770 at Monday's close. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said market participants are awaiting further developments on the war in Iran as negotiations between the US and Iran continue.

items.title: Ronnie Liu: Selangor ruler's message goes beyond Dataran Selangor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Sultan of Selangor Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah's decree on the importance of understanding the Rukun Negara serves as a reminder for politicians to return to the fundamental principles of nation-building, says former Selangor executive councillor Ronnie Liu. Liu said the ruler's message was not merely about attending Dataran Selangor, but about urging politicians to understand the meaning of respect for the nation, the Constitution, the rule of law, the institution of the Rulers and Malaysians. "In my view, His Highness' decree for politicians to attend Dataran Selangor twice a month to be closer to the rakyat and to appreciate the Rukun Negara is not merely symbolic or for formality.

items.title: 19,700 undocumented migrants deported this year amid intensified crackdown
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: A total of 19,700 undocumented migrants have been deported to their home countries so far this year, as the Immigration Department intensifies its crackdown on illegal immigration. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said Indonesians made up the largest group of the deportees with 7,936 individuals. Currently, over 22,000 detainees are being held across 20 immigration depots nationwide, including several temporary facilities.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM MAY 19, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended marginally lower as investors turned more defensive amid escalating geopolitical tensions and weaker regional risk sentiment, with selling pressure focused on selected index heavyweights. In the cryptocurrency market, Bitcoin rose to RM306,517. That wraps up today's Market Pulse.

items.title: Dutch Lady Q1 earnings rises 20pct to RM29.96mil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Dutch Lady Milk Industries Bhd's net profit rose 19.7 per cent to RM29.96 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 FY26) from RM25.03 million in the same period last year. The company said improvement in earnings was due to strong volume performance, the absence of one-off transition-related costs, lower commodity costs, positive exchange rate developments and revenue growth management initiatives. Revenue for the quarter under review rose 6.5 per cent to RM397.84 million versus RM373.4 million a year ago due to higher sales in its liquid milk range, strong momentum in the out-of-home channel and continued growth from new products launched in 2025.

items.title: Automotive industry's TIV rises 14pct to 72,113 units in April
items.summary: Production rose 25 per cent to 70, 611 units in April 2026 from 56,602 units a year earlier. Meanwhile, for the January-April 2026 period, sales inched up two per cent to 254,318 units compared with 250,239 units in the corresponding four-month period last year. Production fell two per cent to 229,988 units from 234,205 units in the same quarter of 2025.

items.title: Number of M'sians detained by Israeli regime rises to 25
items.summary: SEPANG: The Sumud Nusantara Command Centre (SNCC) confirmed that 25 Malaysians who were on 15 ships of Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 were among those detained after dozens of humanitarian aid ships were reportedly intercepted by the Israeli navy (IOF) while en route to Gaza. SNCC director-general Datuk Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby said a total of 40 flotilla ships involving nearly 350 humanitarian activists had been intercepted and raided as of 3pm yesterday (May 19). Sani Araby said SNCC was closely monitoring two possible locations for the transfer of humanitarian aid flotilla activists detained by the Israeli regime, either heading to Greece or the port of Ashdod.

items.title: Johor Media Club regrets incident involving media during Pakatan convention, police report filed
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 19 — The Johor Media Club (KMJ) has lodged a police report over the alleged aggressive conduct by security personnel against media practitioners covering the 2026 Pakatan Harapan (PH) Convention here on Sunday. KMJ said the report lodged at the Johor Bahru Sentral police station earlier was a proactive step following to the alleged incident involving journalists from Johor and Kuala Lumpur. The club said the security personnel were said to have obstructed media practitioners who were present at the Persada Johor International Convention Centre here to cover the political convention.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia ends marginally lower as investors turn defensive
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended marginally lower on Tuesday as investors adopted a more defensive stance amid escalating geopolitical tensions and softer regional risk sentiment. At 5pm, the benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) edged down 0.44 of a point, or 0.03 per cent, to 1,727.27 from yesterday's close of 1,727.71. Turnover was lower at 3.36 billion units worth RM3.24 billion compared with 3.51 billion units worth RM2.84 billion on Monday.

items.title: AirAsia flight turns back to KLIA after technical issue
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: An AirAsia flight bound for Sandakan was forced to turn back to Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) after experiencing a technical issue shortly after takeoff on Sunday. "AirAsia flight AK5192 requested to return to Kuala Lumpur after the pilot detected a technical issue during the flight. The aircraft later landed safely," it said.

items.title: New M'sian passport to roll out June 1 will boast 94 security features
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Immigration Department will begin issuing the next generation of the Malaysian International Passport on June 1, featuring nearly double the security features of the current version to thwart forgery. Immigration Director-General Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the enhanced document will boast 94 security features, a significant upgrade from the 49 features found in the existing passport. "The new Malaysian International Passport will be issued starting June 1, 2026.

items.title: RTD: Over 100 luxury vehicles still dodging road tax
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The Kelantan Road Transport Department (RTD) believes more than 100 luxury vehicles involved in various offences are still being driven on roads following the seizure of 42 vehicles under Op Khas Luxury 2.0. Kelantan RTD director Mohd Misuari Abdullah said the department would continue tracking down the vehicles, most of which were found without valid Motor Vehicle Licences (road tax) and insurance protection. He said the 42 luxury vehicles seized during the four-day operation were valued between RM150,000 and RM500,000 each.

items.title: MHA to deepen collaboration with Media Prima on highway safety
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysia Highway Authority (MHA) is strengthening its strategic cooperation with Media Prima Bhd to enhance highway user safety awareness and expand the dissemination of information related to facilities and traffic management. "With Media Prima's media strength, information, particularly on safety, can be channelled to our highway users. Commenting on highway safety issues, particularly the implementation of Smart Lane, Hadzmir said there are currently 32 Smart Lane locations on highways nationwide, activated mainly during traffic congestion and festive seasons.

items.title: EPF posts 51pct income jump to RM27.73bil in Q1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Employees Provident Fund (EPF)'s total investment income jumped 51 per cent to RM27.73 billion for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 2026) from RM18.31 billion a year ago. According to the EPF, equities income surged 88 per cent to RM20.34 billion in Q1 2026 from RM10.80 billion last year. Meanwhile, fixed income instruments contributed RM6.76 billion, or 24 per cent of total investment income in Q1 2026.

items.title: After mayor's murder, Mexico battles to bring peace
items.summary: THE brazen murder of a small-city mayor shocked Mexico and forced President Claudia Sheinbaum to step up an offensive against all-powerful drug cartels. Residents of the state of Michoacan's second city Uruapan, have resigned themselves to live with violence. Uruapan, a city of more than 300,000 people, is the center of Mexico's multi-billion-dollar avocado industry.

items.title: Blind community set to benefit from flexible digital learning initiative
items.summary: OUM president Professor Datuk Dr Ahmad Izanee Awang (second left) and MAB deputy president Datin Fauziah Ramly (second right) at the MoU signing ceremony between OUM and MAB at the MAB Complex in Brickfields. – NSTP/Aziah Azmee KUALA LUMPUR: Open University Malaysia (OUM) and the Malaysian Association for the Blind (MAB) today took a step towards improving higher education access for the visually impaired community through flexible digital learning. OUM president and vice-chancellor Datuk Professor Dr Ahmad Izanee Awang said technology had made higher education more accessible to the visually impaired community.

items.title: Saifuddin: Immigration Department facing headwinds from sophisticated syndicates
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said increasingly sophisticated syndicates are exploiting technological loopholes and shifting routes, placing growing pressure on the country's Immigration Department. They utilise manipulated passports, forged identities and document-swapping at transit airports to circumvent enforcement," he said during the Immigration Department's 2025 Excellence Service Awards ceremony here today. The country's vast and geographically complex land and maritime borders remain vulnerable to exploitation by syndicates smuggling undocumented workers.

items.title: Concession growth, insulin expansion to drive Pharmaniaga's earnings outlook
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pharmaniaga Bhd expects continued growth in its concession business after the government raised the allocation under the system to RM2.1 billion this year from RM2 billion in 2025. Pharmaniaga managing director Datuk Zulkifli Jafar said actual orders under the concession system are expected to exceed RM2.2 billion this year as more products are added to the Approved Products Purchase List (APPL). He said Pharmaniaga is also strengthening its insulin operations.

items.title: Govt to retable bill limiting PM's tenure in Dewan Rakyat next month
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The government will retable a constitutional amendment to limit a prime minister's tenure to 10 years during the upcoming parliamentary session in June, said Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said. She said the cabinet has finalised discussions on the matter, adding that the bill would require a two-thirds majority in Parliament as it involves a constitutional amendment. "In the June session, we will be retabling the 10-year term limit for the prime minister.

items.title: Refugee policy proposal to be presented to cabinet by mid-June
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Home Ministry is set to present a proposal to the cabinet on the nation's new refugee management framework by mid-June. Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the proposal, which is currently being finalised, follows a significant "game-changing" pilot project currently underway at the Juru Immigration Depot. "Coincidentally, I visited the Juru Immigration Depot yesterday.

items.title: Spanish ex-PM Zapatero under investigation for influence peddling: Court
items.summary: MADRID: Spain's former Socialist Prime Minister Jose Luis Rodriguez Zapatero has been placed under investigation for influence peddling and related crimes, a court said Tuesday. Zapatero, who served as prime minister from 2004 to 2011, has been summoned to testify on June 2, Spain's top criminal court said in a statement. The case centres on the bailout of Venezuela-linked airline Plus Ultra during the Covid-19 pandemic – years after Zapatero had left office.

items.title: Home Ministry to submit Bidor refugee detention centre report to Cabinet in June, says Saifuddin Nasution
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 19 — The Home Ministry (KDN) will present a report on the implementation of the Refugee and Asylum Seeker Special Detention Centre (PPKPPS) in Bidor, Perak, to the Cabinet next month. Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the report would be tabled in line with a directive from Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim during today’s National Economic Action Council (MTEN) meeting. He said he had also asked KDN deputy secretary-general (Security) Datuk Abdul Halim Abdul Rahman to prepare an urgent Cabinet note on the matter.

items.title: Ten Gaza-bound aid vessels in final stretch to destination, arrival expected in nine hours
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Ten Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 vessels, carrying four Malaysians, are continuing their voyage to Gaza and are expected to arrive within nine hours. Sumud Nusantara Command Centre (SNCC) director-general Datuk Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby said the vessels were in Red Zone 2, about 100 nautical miles from the Gaza coast. Sani Araby said the vessels still en route to Gaza were Alcyone, Andros, Cabo Blanco, Don Juan, Elengi, Girolama, Kasr-i-Sadabad, Lina, Sirius and Zefiro.

items.title: Former CM Salleh Said Keruak appointed as Sabah chief inspector of projects
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 19 — Former chief minister Datuk Seri Salleh Said Keruak has been appointed as the Sabah Chief Inspector of Projects. According to a statement from the Chief Minister’s Department, Salleh received his appointment letter from Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Haji Noor at Menara Kinabalu here, today. Salleh, 68, is also the former Member of Parliament for Kota Belud and former Usukan Assemblymen.

items.title: Malaysia riding out oil shock from strength: Standard Chartered
items.summary: Malaysia's gross domestic product (GDP) expanded 5.4 per cent year-on-year in the first quarter (Q1) of 2026, supported mainly by strong services exports and private investment. Services exports and private investment were strong in the Q1," Standard Chartered said in a note. The bank maintained its 2026 GDP growth forecast at 4.5 per cent.

items.title: Male sexual harassment reports rising, says Nancy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sexual harassment cases involving male victims have recorded a significant rise, with more than 1,000 reports received through the complaints counter at the Women, Family and Community Development Ministry since 2024, says Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri. Nancy said physical sexual harassment cases reported by male victims included unwanted physical contact involving certain parts of the body. On the alleged sexual harassment case involving a district police chief in Sarawak, Nancy said the matter was still under police investigation.

items.title: Ebola deaths in eastern Congo rise to 131 as outbreak spreads
items.summary: BUNIA: Twenty-six more suspected Ebola deaths were recorded in 24 hours in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, authorities said on Tuesday, and the head of the World Health Organisation expressed deep concern about the outbreak's spread. There have been 516 suspected cases and 33 confirmed cases in Congo, according to a daily bulletin published by health authorities, and two confirmed cases in neighbouring Uganda. Butembo, a city of hundreds of thousands of people in Congo's North Kivu province, recorded its first two confirmed cases on Monday, Jean-Jacques Muyembe, director of Congo's National Institute for Biomedical Research (INRB), told Reuters.

items.title: Nestle partners SWCorp to ramp up plastic recycling in Kedah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Nestle Malaysia has partnered with SWCorp to strengthen the plastic waste recycling ecosystem in Kedah. The collaboration will support the expansion of Project SAVE (Segregate, Avoid, Value, Educate), Nestle Malaysia's flagship voluntary recycling initiative, through community-led recycling efforts in Kulim. Project SAVE, launched in 2020, is Nestle Malaysia's flagship voluntary recycling programme and the country's largest voluntary EPR initiative.

items.title: Mango clothing founder's son arrested in Spain over tycoon's death
items.summary: BARCELONA: Spanish police said Tuesday they have arrested the eldest son of Mango clothing empire founder Isak Andic over his father's death during a hiking trip in December 2024. Jonathan Andic, who was alone with his 71-year-old father when the retail magnate plunged to his death in the Montserrat mountains near Barcelona, was taken into custody, Catalan regional police said, confirming a report in the daily La Vanguardia newspaper. Investigators initially treated the death as an accident, with early findings suggesting Isak may have slipped.

items.title: Rapid Rail identifies damaged train component as cause of yesterday’s peak-hour Kelana Jaya LRT disruption
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — Rapid Rail has initiated a comprehensive inspection of its train fleet in response to the service disruption on the Kelana Jaya LRT line that occurred yesterday due to damage to a train component at the Kelana Jaya station. In a statement, the company confirmed that its engineering team promptly commenced thorough checks throughout the fleet to verify the safe and optimal functioning of all linear induction motor (LIM) systems. The initial assessment indicated that the disruption was triggered by a damaged LIM cover on train number 92 while stationed at the Kelana Jaya platform.

items.title: Johor media group lodges police report over alleged security interference at convention [WATCH]
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Kelab Media Johor (KMJ) has lodged a police report over an alleged incident involving aggressive conduct by a political party's security personnel against media practitioners covering a convention here on Sunday. The club said security personnel assigned to control crowd movement and escort dignitaries had allegedly acted excessively, resulting in pushing, verbal altercations and disruption to media duties. KMJ said the situation had caused discomfort and raised safety concerns among journalists carrying out their professional duties.

items.title: Woman stabbed in the neck in Kedah undergoes 10-hour surgery, condition stable
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, May 19 — A woman who was allegedly stabbed by a man inside a Mazda 3 car in front of a restaurant in Titi Gajah, Kepala Batas in Alor Setar, Kedah, has successfully undergone a 10-hour surgery. The victim’s sister, who declined to be named, said the woman, who sustained neck injuries, underwent surgery at Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital (HSB) on Monday, Sinar Harian reported today. Kota Setar district police chief Assistant Commissioner Syed Basri Syed Ali said the victim’s condition is now stable.

items.title: Return of three elephants from Osaka zoo cannot be rushed, says deputy minister
items.summary: MERSING, May 19 — The process of bringing home three elephants, Dara, Amoi and Kelat, from Tennoji Zoo in Osaka, Japan, cannot be rushed as it involves several procedures that must be followed, said Deputy Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Syed Ibrahim Syed Noh. There is also an existing agreement between Zoo Taiping and Night Safari (ZTNS) and Tennoji Zoo. Previously, Minister of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup said that proposals from various parties, including non-governmental organisations (NGOs), to bring the elephants home should be given serious consideration.

items.title: Housewife charged again over Facebook comments against King
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 19 — A housewife who was previously granted a discharge not amounting to an acquittal (DNAA) for posting offensive comments on Facebook against the King, His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, was today charged again at the Sessions Court here with the same offence. The fresh charge follows a Federal Court ruling that the words “offensive” and “annoy” in the relevant law are valid and constitutional. On July 7 last year, Suhaila pleaded not guilty to the charge at the Shah Alam Sessions Court.

items.title: Malaysia’s food and goods prices remain stable despite global supply chain pressures, says Akmal Nasrullah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — Malaysia’s overall price trend for goods and food items remains stable despite mounting global supply chain disruptions and rising energy prices, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said today. Speaking after the National Economic Action Council (MTEN) meeting, Akmal Nasrullah said Putrajaya’s continued monitoring and intervention measures had so far helped prevent broader inflationary pressures from spilling into daily household spending. The average price of selected food items that were monitored from May 11 until May 17 hovered between -3.8 per cent to 4.7 per cent compared to last week.

items.title: Economy minister: Govt maintains RM300 diesel aid, adds RM100 interim assistance as global fuel prices rise
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — The government will maintain the RM300 monthly BUDI MADANI Diesel cash assistance while introducing an additional interim RM100 aid payment for eligible recipients amid rising global fuel prices and growing supply chain uncertainty. Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the measures were part of Putrajaya’s targeted intervention strategy to shield lower-income groups and vulnerable sectors from the effects of the ongoing global supply crisis. Announcing the decision after today’s National Economic Action Council (MTEN) meeting, Akmal Nasrullah said the government remained focused on ensuring the rakyat were not left to bear rising costs alone.

items.title: ‘Come once a week’: Selangor Sultan asks Ronnie Liu, DAP rep to visit Rukun Negara monument over pig farming controversy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — Sultan of Selangor Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah has called on all Selangor assemblymen to visit Dataran Selangor twice a month to read and understand the Rukun Negara displayed on a newly built monument plaque. His Royal Highness said the plaque, placed in front of the Selangor state flagpole, was built and installed by the Selangor Public Works Department together with benches for visitors. Sultan Sharafuddin said he had fully funded the cost of the Rukun Negara monument plaque and the benches.

items.title: Zambry: SPM remains main entry requirement for IPTA admissions
items.summary: KUALA BERANG, May 19 — Admission to public higher education institutions (IPTA) through the Unit Pusat Universiti (UPU) Online is still based on the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM), said Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Zambry Abd Kadir. He said the government, through a Cabinet decision, has set that the basic requirement for admission to higher education institutions via UPU Online is SPM, regardless of the stream or type of school attended. The requirements under UPU Online are based on SPM.

items.title: Defence minister: Navy to decide replacement for cancelled Norwegian Naval Strike Missile system
items.summary: LUMUT, May 19 —The Ministry of Defence (Mindef) has left it to the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) to assess alternatives to replace the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) system, after its supply was cancelled by a Norwegian defence contractor. Mohamed Khaled said replacing the NSM system would involve additional costs, including training personnel and integrating a new system with existing platforms. “Then there are training costs and the need to ensure integration between the new system and the existing combat system.

items.title: Sabah probes suspected ivory poaching after mutilated Bornean elephant found in Tongod forest reserve
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 19 — Sabah authorities are investigating the death of a Bornean elephant found mutilated in a forest reserve in Tongod, believed to be linked to ivory poaching. State Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Datuk Jafry Ariffin said he was deeply concerned by the discovery of the dead elephant within the nursery forest area of the Sungai Pinangah Forest Reserve. According to reports, the Kinabatangan Wildlife team received a complaint at approximately 8.25am on May 18, 2026 regarding a dead elephant suspected to be linked to wildlife crime.

items.title: Amirudin calls for grassroots cooperation to curb cable theft disrupting digital services in Selangor
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 19 — Local communities have been urged to play a more active role in providing early information to help authorities combat cable theft and vandalism involving telecommunications infrastructure in Selangor. Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said public cooperation is crucial to ensure immediate action can be taken to prevent acts of sabotage, which not only damage public facilities but also disrupt the public’s digital access. As I mentioned, digital infrastructure is important for sustaining daily life in the state of Selangor.

items.title: Penang 75pc ready for snap polls, says Chow Kon Yeow
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 19 — Penang is about 75 per cent ready to face snap polls, said Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow. He said there is no fixed point at which a state can be considered fully ready for elections, whether at 80 or 100 per cent. ““If you ask me, I always give the answer 75 per cent, not completely prepared, but educated prepared if there is a possibility,” he said when he was asked whether the state is ready to face snap polls.

items.title: Zambry: BN will stay with unity govt until end of mandate
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — Barisan Nasional (BN) will continue to preserve understanding in the Unity Government until the end of the administration’s mandate to ensure national stability and the well-being of the people, said its secretary-general Datuk Seri Zambry Abd Kadir. Touching on developments in Johor, Zambry said BN respects the stance voiced by the state’s BN regarding the direction and preparations for the upcoming state election. Last Saturday, Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced that the coalition would contest all 56 state assembly seats.

items.title: MBI Selangor denies involvement in Bernam Maritime Industrial Park launch, says use of its name and logo unauthorised
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 19 — Menteri Besar Selangor (Incorporated) (MBI Selangor) has denied any involvement in the organisation, management, ownership, promotion or operation of the Bernam Maritime Industrial Park project and its launch ceremony, as circulated by Bernam Shipyard Holdings Sdn Bhd. “MBI Selangor has conducted an initial check with the Sabak Bernam District Council (MDSB) and, based on the information currently available, there is no official confirmation linking MBI Selangor and MDSB to the project or the launch ceremony. “MBI Selangor views seriously the unauthorised use of its name, logo and branding elements in promotional and publicity materials related to the event and project.

items.title: Sateerah Hassan quits as Putrajaya PKR Women deputy chief
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — Sateerah Hassan has confirmed her resignation as deputy chief of the Women’s wing of Parti Keadilan Rakyat’s (PKR) Putrajaya division. Putrajaya PKR Women chief Fasyarini Azahari said the party acknowledged Sateerah’s decision and respected her choice, according to a report published in Sinar Harian today. She said the party appreciated Sateerah’s contributions, commitment and service throughout her time in the leadership line-up.

items.title: Malaysia seeks RM1b from Norwegian firm over cancelled missile export licence, says Khaled
items.summary: LUMUT, May 19 — The Ministry of Defence (Mindef) has issued a notice of demand to a Norwegian manufacturer following the cancellation of an export licence for the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) anti-ship and land-attack missile system. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the notice, seeking compensation for both direct and indirect losses, was sent to the company today. He said the Norwegian government had unilaterally revoked the export licence without prior discussions with Malaysia, despite the government having fulfilled all contractual obligations.

items.title: Ex-Perlis MB’s son acquitted of false claims charge involving RM19,000
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — The Sessions Court in Kangar, Perlis, today acquitted and discharged former Perlis Menteri Besar Mohd Shukri Ramli’s son, Mohd Syafeeq Mohd Shukri, 37, of a charge involving the submission of a false claim worth more than RM19,000, without calling for his defence. According to a report published by Berita Harian , Judge Datuk Nu’aman Mahmud Zuhudi made the ruling after finding that the prosecution had failed to establish a prima facie case against the accused at the close of the prosecution’s case. In delivering the judgment, Nu’aman said the court’s role at the end of the prosecution stage was to determine whether a prima facie case had been proven.

items.title: ‘A leopard cannot change its spots’: Lim Guan Eng labels Khairy, Umno ‘fake progressives’ over defence of Dr Akmal’s politics
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — DAP adviser Lim Guan Eng today accused Khairy Jamaluddin and Umno of being “fake progressives” after the former minister defended Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh’s style of politics. In a statement, Lim said Khairy’s assertion that Akmal’s political approach remained necessary in Umno was disappointing, especially after previously positioning himself as a progressive and non-racial politician. Lim said Khairy appeared to suggest that Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi tolerated Akmal’s controversial “culture war politics” because of what he described as a “clear and present danger” from the political right dominated by Perikatan Nasional.

items.title: Former Sabah Water Dept director found guilty of money laundering, wife and deputy acquitted
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 19 — The Sessions Court here has found former Sabah Water Department director Ag Mohd Tahir Mohd Talib guilty of 12 charges of money laundering. Two others — Ag Mohd Tahir’s wife Fauziah Piut and former deputy director Lim Lam Beng @ Lim Chee Hong — were discharged and acquitted of all their charges. Fauziah had 19 charges while Lim faced four charges.

items.title: Police detain 15 over dangerous flare stunt ride in Bukit Bintang linked to Dengkil clothing shop
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — Police have detained 15 people, including a woman, less than 24 hours after they allegedly ignited flares and rode dangerously around Bukit Bintang in an incident that went viral on social media. According to The Star, Dang Wangi police chief ACP Sazalee Adam said the suspects, aged between 17 and 30, were arrested in Batu Caves, Shah Alam, Dengkil and Kajang during an operation conducted between noon and 8pm yesterday. He reportedly said that police seized two electric scrambler motorcycles and a four-wheel-drive vehicle believed to have been used in the stunt.

items.title: Penang volunteer fire brigades lodge police report over Facebook claims linking them to money laundering
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 19 — Penang volunteer fire brigades have lodged a police report against a Facebook user over allegations linking the organisations to money laundering and tax evasion activities. He said the comment made by “Yoong Hai” also linked donations made to volunteer fire brigades to money laundering and tax evasion schemes. Meanwhile, H’ng said there are 38 volunteer fire brigade teams in Penang.

items.title: Over 20 villagers band together to capture 300kg crocodile in KK near Sabah Legislative House
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — More than 20 villagers joined forces to capture a crocodile weighing about 300 kilogrammes in Kota Kinabalu earlier today. The crocodile had reportedly been seen surfacing near the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) beach over the past few weeks, raising concern among residents and visitors. Meanwhile, Kota Kinabalu Civil Defence Force (APM) officer Capt (PA) Aizex Junior said the District Operations Control Centre received a call at 8.02am.

items.title: Penang gets tough on litterbugs: Body cams, 24-hour enforcement rolled out under new community service order scheme
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 19 — Officers equipped with body cameras will be deployed as part of Penang’s new Community Service Order (PKM) enforcement against litterbugs from July 1 onwards. State Local Government and Town and Country Planning Committee chairman H’ng Mooi Lye said enforcement action will also be carried out round-the-clock. He said the use of body cameras, along with photographic and video recordings, will serve as evidence during enforcement operations.

items.title: Malaysia beats growth forecasts despite oil price surge above US$100, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — Malaysia’s economic performance has continued to outperform initial forecasts, despite global pressures and rising oil prices, according to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said that economic projections had earlier been based on oil prices of around US$70 (RM278) per barrel, but these have since climbed to above US$100 amid shifting global conditions. “When earlier economic projections were made, oil prices were around US$70 per barrel.

items.title: No cuts to programmes: Govt keeps sports funding intact, athletes’ welfare top priority, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said that there will be no financial cuts to sports programmes despite ongoing economic pressures. He said the government remains committed to safeguarding the country’s sporting development. “Despite financial constraints, I have assured the Cabinet that sports programmes and athlete welfare will be fully safeguarded.

items.title: Malaysia targets 902 clinics for integrated lung health rollout by end-2026, says Health Minister
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 19 — Malaysia has stepped up its efforts to translate the World Health Assembly’s Lung Health Resolution into practical action by pursuing an integrated healthcare approach and forging strong public-private partnerships to improve respiratory health outcomes nationwide. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad said that over the past year, Malaysia has focused on implementing the resolution through two key strategies: strengthening integrated lung health services at the primary care level and operationalising a whole-of-society approach under the Lung Health Initiative Malaysia. He said this in a speech for the Lung Health Forum: From Resolution to Action - Government Leadership on Lung Health, to mark the 79th World Health Assembly (WHA79), which was shared by the Ministry of Health (MOH) here today.
